data.title: OSS projects $59M-$61M revenue for 2025 with 20% growth in OSS segment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: One Stop Systems, Inc. (OSS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Michael Knowles emphasized the company's progress in its multiyear transformation plan, highlighting sequential consolidated growth throughout 2024 and a return to year-over-year revenue growth in Q4. He noted strong adoption in both defense and commercial markets, driven by demand for AI, machine learning, and autonomous applications.
  • Customer-funded development revenue increased by 118% to $3.7 million in 2024, which management views as a precursor to larger multiyear production contracts.
  • The OSS segment reported a book-to-bill ratio of 1.14 for the year, with expectations for this ratio to rise to approximately 1.2 in 2025.
  • CFO Daniel Gabel reported consolidated revenue of $15.1 million for Q4, representing a 15.1% year-over-year increase. He also explained onetime charges of $1.2 million for a development contract and $7.1 million for inventory adjustments to legacy programs, which impacted overall profitability.

Outlook

  • Management projected consolidated revenue for 2025 in the range of $59 million to $61 million, with the OSS segment expected to generate approximately $30 million, reflecting over 20% year-over-year growth.
  • The company anticipates being EBITDA breakeven for the full year 2025, with improved profitability expected in the second half of the year.
  • Key growth opportunities for 2025 include transitioning development programs into sales, particularly in data center, health care, aerospace, and defense markets.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated gross margin for Q4 was reported at 15.7%, but excluding onetime charges, the adjusted gross margin was 23.8%. The OSS segment's adjusted gross margin stood at 26.8%.
  • The company reported a GAAP net loss of $3.1 million or $0.15 per share, which included the $1.2 million development contract charge.
  • Non-GAAP metrics showed a net loss of $2.6 million or $0.12 per share, while adjusted EBITDA was reported as a loss of $2.3 million.
  • Cash and short-term investments as of December 31, 2024, were $10 million, with no borrowings on the revolving credit line and a $1 million term loan balance.

Q&A

  • Brian Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners, inquired about the OSS segment's gross margin pressure. CFO Gabel noted variability due to product mix and highlighted that gross margins were 36.4% for the year, excluding inventory charges, aligning with historical levels.
  • Kinstlinger questioned potential pipeline impact due to order delays. CEO Knowles indicated stability in the $1 billion pipeline, with approximately $200 million addressable in 2025.
  • Maxwell Michaelis, Lake Street Capital Markets, asked about growth distribution between commercial and defense markets. Knowles stated that growth is balanced, with notable traction in medical imaging for the commercial segment.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on gross margin recovery and order execution timelines. Concerns were raised about potential delays in government procurement processes.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing stable pipeline opportunities and strategic positioning. However, responses during Q&A reflected a measured approach to addressing uncertainties in order timing and budgetary impacts.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue of $15.1 million marked an improvement from Q3's $13.7 million, driven by double-digit growth in both OSS and Bressner segments.
  • Gross margin declined from Q3 due to onetime charges and product mix, with adjusted margins showing improvement.
  • Management's tone remained consistent, with an emphasis on long-term pipeline conversion and strategic growth initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include delays in government spending and procurement processes, potential budgetary constraints, and the impact of inventory-related adjustments.
  • Management is focused on mitigating risks through strategic customer engagement and prioritizing high-margin opportunities.

Final Takeaway

One Stop Systems closed 2024 with strong sequential growth and significant progress in its transformation strategy, despite onetime charges impacting profitability. With a robust pipeline and strategic focus on high-growth markets such as AI and defense, the company projects $59 million to $61 million in 2025 revenue, supported by over 20% growth in the OSS segment. Management remains optimistic about the revenue conversion of customer-funded development programs and is strategically positioning the company for long-term profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: TILT Holdings targets Q2 2025 for strategic divestitures and European vape certification
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TILT Holdings Inc. (TLLTF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Tim Conder highlighted that TILT Holdings has been undergoing a strategic review of its plant-touching business, resulting in the announcement of a definitive agreement to divest its Massachusetts retail stores for $2 million. The company expects regulatory approval for this transaction in Q2 2025 and plans to divest its remaining plant-touching assets this year.
  • Conder emphasized the company’s strategic focus on its Jupiter Research business, aiming to position it as a leader in vape hardware technology. He announced leadership changes, including elevating Khalid Al Naser to Chief Commercial Officer of Jupiter.
  • The CEO mentioned, "We are doubling down on our commitment to Jupiter... to redefine the standards for innovation and service in the vaporization space."
  • Interim CFO Brad Hoch reported Q4 revenue of $24.6 million, with Jupiter contributing $17.4 million. He noted that the decline in year-over-year revenue for Jupiter was due to a shift to a commission-based invoicing model for some customers.
  • Hoch also highlighted improvements in gross margin, which increased to 22% in Q4, driven by the commission-based model.

Outlook

  • Conder provided updates on upcoming product launches from Jupiter, including the QMID vaporization device, which is expected to be commercialized in Q2 2025 upon receiving European Union medical certification. He described this as a "huge opportunity" as it would be the first medically certified handheld device in the region.
  • The company anticipates completing the divestiture of remaining plant-touching assets by the end of 2025, with discussions and negotiations actively underway.
  • Management also signaled a continued focus on addressing debt challenges and exploring opportunities for refinancing as part of its strategic pivot.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 2024 was $24.6 million, compared to $27 million in Q3. Jupiter's revenue increased sequentially to $17.4 million but declined year-over-year due to a transition to a commission-based model for certain customers.
  • Gross margin improved to 22%, up from 14% in Q3, attributed to the new invoicing structure in Jupiter’s operations. Adjusted gross margin was 24%.
  • Operating expenses for Q4 decreased to $7.3 million, reflecting cost reduction efforts.
  • Net loss for the quarter was reported at $41.4 million, driven by intangible asset impairments and fair value measurements. Adjusted EBITDA improved to $500,000 from a negative $1.6 million in Q3.
  • Cash used in operations during Q4 was $500,000, with cash and equivalents at $4.3 million as of December 31, 2024.

Q&A

  • Aaron Grey, Alliance Global Partners: Asked about mitigating tariff impacts from Chinese imports and potential shifts in production. CEO Conder responded, "We have already been shifting production to Indonesia to preemptively address tariff concerns, and we plan to have the majority of production outside of China by year-end."
  • Pablo Zuanic, Zuanic & Associates: Inquired about the confidence in completing plant-touching asset divestitures given market challenges. Conder noted ongoing discussions and highlighted the support from Innovative Industrial Properties (IIPR) in facilitating these transactions.
  • Grey also asked about Jupiter’s growth opportunities. Conder elaborated that Jupiter is expanding its supplier base to enhance product offerings and ensure customer needs are met, particularly in sustainability and innovation.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about tariff impacts and the execution of the asset divestiture strategy. Their tone was cautious but optimistic about management’s steps to address these challenges.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic focus and leadership changes. However, responses in the Q&A revealed a more measured approach to addressing financial and operational concerns.
  • Compared to Q3, management’s tone showed increased confidence in strategic execution but acknowledged ongoing challenges in market conditions and balance sheet restructuring.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased to $24.6 million in Q4 from $27 million in Q3, largely due to the Jupiter invoicing model transition. However, gross margin improved significantly from 14% to 22%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA shifted from a negative $1.6 million in Q3 to $500,000 in Q4, reflecting operational efficiencies.
  • The sentiment from analysts remained cautious, with a continued focus on divestitures and debt management. Management’s confidence increased as they reported meaningful progress in their strategic initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing market pressures in Massachusetts and Pennsylvania, including pricing challenges and market saturation.
  • Analysts raised questions about the ability to execute divestitures in a competitive and challenging market environment. Management emphasized active discussions and partnerships to mitigate these risks.
  • Debt obligations remain a significant challenge, with management working closely with creditors to address sustainability issues.

Final Takeaway

TILT Holdings is undergoing a transformative period, focusing on divesting its plant-touching assets to streamline operations and prioritize its Jupiter Research business. With leadership changes and a renewed focus on innovation, the company aims to capitalize on growth opportunities in the vape hardware market, including the European Union. Despite ongoing financial challenges, management expressed confidence in its ability to execute strategic initiatives and address operational hurdles in 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Peraso anticipates mmWave revenue growth exceeding 2024's full year in Q1 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Peraso Inc. (PRSO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ron Glibbery highlighted a 100% year-over-year revenue growth for Q4, driven by the execution of end-of-life memory IC product shipments. He emphasized the company’s focus on expanding its customer base for mmWave solutions, which are now utilized in 60 commercial products across 11 customers.

  • CEO Glibbery announced a $3.6 million purchase order from a strategic customer in the fixed wireless access (FWA) market, reflecting growing momentum in the mmWave segment. He also pointed out the traction of the DUNE platform in dense urban environments and its increasing adoption in diverse geographies.

  • CFO James Sullivan reported Q4 total net revenue of $3.7 million, with a GAAP gross margin of 56.3% and a non-GAAP gross margin of 71.6%. He noted an improvement in operating expenses and cash position, with a sequential cash increase to $3.3 million.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates Q1 2025 total net revenue between $3.6 million and $4 million. CEO Glibbery expects mmWave revenue in Q1 2025 to surpass total mmWave revenue for the entirety of 2024, driven by increasing production orders.

  • CEO Glibbery expressed optimism regarding the ongoing adoption of mmWave solutions in both the FWA and tactical military markets. He also highlighted potential contributions from the U.S. BEAD program, which could accelerate adoption of mmWave technology.

Financial Results

  • Q4 total net revenue was $3.7 million, slightly below the prior quarter’s $3.8 million, but up from $1.8 million in Q4 2023. Full year 2024 revenue reached $14.6 million compared to $13.7 million in 2023.

  • GAAP net loss for Q4 2024 was $1.6 million, with a non-GAAP net loss of $0.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA improved to negative $0.4 million.

  • The company’s non-GAAP gross margin for Q4 2024 significantly improved to 71.6%, driven by favorable product mix and reduced inventory write-downs.

Q&A

  • David Williams, Benchmark: Asked about the timeline for fulfilling the $3.6 million order and future customer demand trends. CEO Glibbery noted a linear fulfillment through 2025 and highlighted a more diversified customer base as a strength.

  • Kevin Liu, K. Liu & Company: Inquired about mmWave revenue scaling in 2025 and military market opportunities. CFO Sullivan projected a sequential revenue increase, while CEO Glibbery indicated that new military engagements might convert to production by 2026.

  • Analysts raised questions about the BEAD program's impact. CEO Glibbery expressed optimism for potential contributions starting in 2025, citing the shift towards a technology-neutral approach.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a positive tone regarding the company’s progress in diversifying its customer base and the traction in mmWave technology.

  • Management’s sentiment was confident, particularly in addressing the growth potential of mmWave solutions and the positive shift in the FWA market dynamics.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was a noticeable increase in management’s confidence, supported by new purchase orders and improved financial performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The Q4 2024 gross margin improved significantly compared to Q3 2024, attributed to a favorable product mix and reduced inventory write-downs.

  • Management shifted focus towards scaling mmWave production, signaling a transition from reliance on memory IC revenues.

  • Analyst discussions in the current quarter were more focused on mmWave growth and military applications, compared to the prior quarter’s emphasis on overcoming inventory challenges.

  • Sentiment around the BEAD program evolved positively, with management highlighting its potential to drive mmWave adoption.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified the potential challenges in scaling production for large mmWave orders and maintaining profitability amidst ongoing market shifts.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing and material impact of military engagements, as well as the pace of adoption for mmWave solutions in the FWA market.

Final Takeaway

Peraso Inc.’s strategic shift towards mmWave technology is gaining momentum, supported by strong purchase orders and a diversified customer base. With expectations for Q1 2025 mmWave revenues to exceed the full year 2024, the company is positioned for significant growth. While challenges remain in scaling production and navigating new market dynamics, management’s optimism and improving financial metrics reflect a positive outlook for the year ahead.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wholesale egg prices drop, but retail rates could take time to catch up
data.description:
Wholesale egg prices have been declining, given the rapid recovery in supply and no significant bird flu outbreaks this month. But it's unclear when retail prices will reflect this decline.

According to data from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, national wholesale egg prices fell to $4.15 per dozen as of March 14, compared to $6.85 a week earlier. That's about half the levels seen at the start of the month.

"However, these declines have yet to be reflected at store shelves and, until they are, demand is expected to remain dampened," the agency said. "Grocers have made progress in maintaining a more consistent stock of shell eggs, but some of this is due to many offering little in the way of price incentives, which helped to reduce or eliminate much of the panic and opportunity buying seen in February."

Last month, consumers on average paid a record $5.90 for a dozen large grade-A eggs, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. That's 22% higher than the previous record of $4.82 per dozen in January 2023.

Wholesale prices will likely fall further, given "extreme pressure" from market dynamics, Karyn Rispoli, managing editor at Expana, told CNBC. Expana tracks agricultural commodity prices.

But there's generally a lag of at least 2-3 weeks between a change in wholesale costs and resulting retail pricing. And retailers ultimately choose "how closely they want to track wholesale prices," Rispoli added.

To note, the Department of Justice is investigating the surge in egg prices, and looking into whether large producers have conspired to raise prices or limit supply.

Consumer advocacy group Food & Water Watch has claimed that Cal-Maine Foods (NASDAQ:CALM) raised prices during the bird flu outbreak in 2023 despite its flocks being unaffected. The price hikes allegedly helped the largest U.S. egg producer earn over $1B in "windfall profits" that year.

data.title: KE Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.16 misses by $0.09, revenue of $4.3B beats by $530M
data.description:

data.title: Shiny at $3,000? SA subscribers called the latest gold rally
data.description:
Just five weeks ago, Seeking Alpha's Wall Street Breakfast ran a poll on whether gold was a buy at current levels as the shiny metal set its sights on $3,000/oz. Bullishness won out in the survey, and bullishness was delivered. Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) closed above the $3K level for the first time on Friday, and continues to extend its historic rally, up another 1% overnight to $3,026/oz.

Note: The WSB polls continue each week, so stay on top of the latest results, like yesterday's question on the future trajectory of Nvidia following the recent stock selloff.

What's driving the gold rally? Only one quarter into the year, the yellow metal is up 15% YTD. It's prompting Wall Street to continue increasing its price targets on bullion, with UBS becoming the latest to raise projections as investors seek safety amid geopolitical and tariff risks. Those aren't the only factors, with global central banks ramping up purchases to diversify their reserves amid concerns over growth, inflation and deficit expansion.

Where to gain exposure? "What about the miners? HUI, the Gold Bugs Index, is +29% since Jan. 1!," Miners to the Moon writes in the latest gold article on Seeking Alpha. "Stick to the underlying!" small supernova responds. "The miners have a) operating costs going up in tandem with the metal, and b) all the operational and political risks; and specific company risk, and c) they've historically been terrible stewards of capital. Just play the metal. Use leverage and/or derivatives on the metal (easy, cheap and liquid) if you want extra juice akin to the miners."

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously as interest rate decision nears
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Tuesday as the traders awaited the Federal Reserve's rate decision slated for Wednesday.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.05%.

The major averages ended on top on Monday as market players assessed the latest retail sales report and prepared themselves for Wednesday’s FOMC rate decision.

"The rebound from Thursday’s S&P 500 correction continued yesterday, even if the sting in the tail was a fresh decline in U.S. tech mega caps," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The upbeat tone was helped by the absence of negative trade headlines and was if anything helped by a Bloomberg report that U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer was reinstating a more process-oriented approach ahead of the expected April 2 tariff announcements, Reid added. 

The two-day FOMC meeting will commence today, with the monetary policy decision scheduled to be announced on Wednesday. It is widely expected that the Fed will hold rates steady.

On the economic calendar for Tuesday, the February Housing Starts and Permits and February Import/Export Prices are scheduled.

The Export Price Index is expected to come in at -0.2% M/M, while the Import Price Index is forecasted at -0.1% M/M.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Oil climbs as Mideast tension back in focus, eyes on Trump-Putin talk
data.description:
Oil prices rose on Tuesday, as tensions in the Middle East have resurfaced, with traders also keeping a close eye on the outcome of talks between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

Nymex crude (CL1:COM) +1.44% to $68.55 a barrel, and Brent crude (CO1:COM) +1.29% to $71.98.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI).

Middle East tensions escalated once again lately with U.S. strikes on the Houthis, while the Houthis retaliated by targeting a U.S. aircraft carrier. 

Unsurprisingly, the Trump administration is taking a more hawkish stance against Iran, having tightened oil sanctions since taking office, ING analysts said.

Trump is meanwhile scheduled to talk to President Putin today about a proposed ceasefire.

“Energy markets will be watching closely for any progress -- particularly whether a potential peace deal might include the resumption of some Russian energy flows. This would be more impactful for natural gas rather than oil, given that the scope to increase natural gas flows is much bigger relative to oil.”

Elsewhere, despite a 1.3% rebound in the Bloomberg Commodity Index, hedge funds continued their third consecutive week of net selling, still reacting to the late-February correction that saw the index drop 4.6% before recovering, Saxo Bank reported.

The total net long across 27 major futures contracts has declined by 44% during this time after hitting a 2-1/2-year high, led by selling across energy and grains.

Energy: The sector’s selloff extended to a seventh week, with the net long position down 62% to 224K contracts during this time—half the three-year average. 

Metals: Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) remained steady before surging to $3,000 per ounce at week’s end, with minimal hedge fund activity seen during the reporting week, Saxo's Head of Commodity Strategy, Ole Hansen noted. 

Silver (+2.4%) and copper (+4.6%) gains contributed to increased net longs in both.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Elbit Systems Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.71, revenue of $1.93B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Elbit Systems press release (NASDAQ:ESLT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.66 beats by $0.71.
  • Revenue of $1.93B (+18.4% Y/Y) beats by $150M.

  • Backlog of orders for the year ended December 31, 2024 totaled $22.6 billion, as compared to $17.8 billion as of December 31, 2023.

  • Approximately 65% of the current backlog is attributable to orders from outside of Israel. Approximately 57% of the current backlog is scheduled to be performed during 2025 and 2026.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities in the year ended December 31, 2024 was $534.6 million, as compared to $113.7 million in the year ended December 31, 2023.
